name: James Earl Jones
abstract: James Earl Jones (January 17, 1931 – September 9, 2024) was an American actor. A pioneer for Black actors in the entertainment industry, he was acclaimed for his performances on stage and screen.

about_quotes.speaker: Erskine Hawkins
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Right. It just opened again with James Earl Jones this time. Right? [INTERVIEWEE] Right. Now, you can see how long since I've been there. I saw Othello then with Paul Robeson.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: Although there was a film that came out with that in mind. James Earl Jones played the President.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
